- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# ROADMAP - Migrate hybrid e2e tests into hybrid_sim

## Goal

`hybrid_sim` owns the cross-component contract tests (QEMU + vsim + plugin
spawned together). `hybrid_vsim` keeps only vsim-internal unit tests.
`hybrid_qemu` is unaffected.

## Acceptance (whole roadmap)

From a fresh clone:
```
./build.sh           # qemu + vsim + plugin
./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e from hybrid_sim build dir
```
runs the same set of e2e cases that `ctest -L hybrid-e2e` runs today from
`hybrid_vsim/build/`, with no skips caused by the workspace layout (no
hardcoded `/local/nick/...`).

## Ground rules

- One feature per session. Tidy First: every feature here is **structural**
  (no behavior change). Each commit message starts with `structural:`.
- After each feature, both old and new ctest invocations must produce the
  same pass/skip set until the registration is migrated (F4).
- No mixing with behavioral changes (e.g., new test cases) until the
  roadmap is complete.

---

## F1 - Make e2e scripts path-agnostic

**State:** TODO

**Description:** Remove every `REPO_ROOT`-derived default and the
hardcoded `/local/nick/qemu_v5/...` from the e2e scripts. All artifact
paths come in via env: `VSIM_BIN`, `VSIM_QEMU`, `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_ELF`.
Scripts exit 77 (CTest skip) if any required env var is unset or its
target is missing. Update `HybridConfig.cmake` to pass those env vars
explicitly via `set_tests_properties(... ENVIRONMENT ...)` or in the
`COMMAND` line, sourced from CMake variables that already know the build
layout.

**Key files:**
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_corrupt_state.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` (add ENVIRONMENT property per test)

**Dependencies:** none

**Acceptance:** `cd hybrid_vsim/build && ctest -L hybrid-e2e
--output-on-failure` produces the identical pass/skip set as before this
feature, with no script using `REPO_ROOT`.

---

## F2 - Top-level CMake skeleton in hybrid_sim

**State:** TODO

**Description:** Add a minimal `hybrid_sim/CMakeLists.txt` that does
`project(hybrid_sim NONE)`, `enable_testing()`, and exposes cache vars
`HYBRID_QEMU_BIN`, `HYBRID_VSIM_BIN`, `HYBRID_PLUGIN_SO` (defaulting to
the workspace-built paths). Extend `build.sh` so a new step configures
this top-level project into `build/hybrid_sim/`. No tests registered
here yet.

**Key files:**
- `hybrid_sim/CMakeLists.txt` (new)
- `hybrid_sim/build.sh`

**Dependencies:** F1 (so the env contract is already stable)

**Acceptance:** `./build.sh` succeeds and produces
`build/hybrid_sim/CMakeCache.txt`. `ctest --test-dir build/hybrid_sim`
runs and reports zero tests.

---

## F3 - Move e2e scripts to hybrid_sim/tests/

**State:** TODO

**Description:** `git mv hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/{roundtrip_e2e,rt_shared_mem,rt_corrupt_state,rt_no_qemu_binary,rt_gdbstub_timeout}.sh
hybrid_sim/tests/`. Update `HybridConfig.cmake` to point its
`COMMAND bash` at the new locations (via a relative path that resolves
from the vsim build dir, e.g. `${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../tests/...`, with a
`HYBRID_SIM_ROOT` variable so the relation is named not magic).

**Key files:**
- `hybrid_sim/tests/*.sh` (moved)
- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`

**Dependencies:** F1, F2

**Acceptance:** `cd hybrid_vsim/build && ctest -L hybrid-e2e` produces
the identical pass/skip set; no script remains under
`hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/` (only `*.test.cpp` and helpers stay).

---

## F4 - Migrate ctest registration into hybrid_sim

**State:** TODO

**Description:** Lift the e2e `add_test(...)` block (currently
`hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake:99-150`) into
`hybrid_sim/CMakeLists.txt`. Drive the `VSIM_RT_FIXTURES` loop from
there. Delete the e2e block from `HybridConfig.cmake`; vsim CMake stops
registering anything labeled `hybrid-e2e`. Update `build.sh test` to run
`ctest --test-dir build/hybrid_sim -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure`
instead of cd-ing into the vsim build dir.

**Dependencies:** F3

**Acceptance:** `./build.sh test` runs the full e2e suite from the

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build the hybrid TCG plugin (libhybrid_handoff.so) against hybrid_qemu's
# headers. Output stays next to the source under hybrid_vsim/.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PLUGIN_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin"
QEMU_SRC="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"

if [[ ! -f "$PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile" ]]; then
  echo "missing $PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "$QEMU_SRC/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h" ]]; then
  echo "missing $QEMU_SRC/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h - hybrid_qemu submodule not initialized" >&2
  exit 1
fi

make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_SRC"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Driver: build qemu + vsim, then add build dirs to PATH.
3	# Source this script to affect your current shell's PATH:
4	#   source ./setup.sh
5	
6	if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
7	  _SETUP_SRC="${(%):-%x}"
8	elif [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
9	  _SETUP_SRC="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
10	else
11	  _SETUP_SRC="$0"
12	fi
13	
14	# Wrap the body in a function so strict-mode options stay local and don't
15	# leak into the caller's interactive shell (which would break zsh prompts
16	# under nounset).
17	_hybrid_setup_main() {
18	  if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
19	    emulate -L sh
20	    setopt err_exit no_unset pipe_fail
21	  else
22	    local -
23	    set -euo pipefail
24	  fi
25	
26	  local ROOT QEMU_BIN_DIR VSIM_BIN_DIR PLUGIN_SO
27	  ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$_SETUP_SRC")" && pwd)"
28	  QEMU_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/build/qemu"
29	  VSIM_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/build"
30	  PLUGIN_SO="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so"
31	
32	  step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
33	  ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Driver: build qemu + vsim, then add build dirs to PATH.
# Source this script to affect your current shell's PATH:
#   source ./setup.sh

if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${(%):-%x}"
elif [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
else
  _SETUP_SRC="$0"
fi

# Wrap the body in a function so strict-mode options stay local and don't
# leak into the caller's interactive shell (which would break zsh prompts
# under nounset).
_hybrid_setup_main() {
  if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
    emulate -L sh
    setopt err_exit no_unset pipe_fail
  else
    local -
    set -euo pipefail
  fi

  local ROOT QEMU_BIN_DIR VSIM_BIN_DIR PLUGIN_SO
  ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$_SETUP_SRC")" && pwd)"
  QEMU_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/build/qemu"
  VSIM_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/build"
  PLUGIN_SO="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so"

  step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
  ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
  err()  { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

  step "1/3 building qemu"
  "$ROOT/build_qemu.sh"
  ok "qemu -> $QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"

  step "2/3 building vsim"
  "$ROOT/build_vsim.sh"
  ok "vsim -> $VSIM_BIN_DIR/vsim"

  step "3/3 building hybrid plugin"
  "$ROOT/build_plugin.sh"
  ok "plugin -> $PLUGIN_SO"

  step "PATH: prepending build dirs"
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$QEMU_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$QEMU_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$VSIM_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$VSIM_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  ok "PATH updated (this shell only)"

  step "env: exporting VSIM_QEMU / VSIM_PLUGIN for e2e scripts"
  export VSIM_QEMU="$QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"
  export VSIM_PLUGIN="$PLUGIN_SO"
  ok "VSIM_QEMU=$VSIM_QEMU"
  ok "VSIM_PLUGIN=$VSIM_PLUGIN"

  if [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ] && [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" = "${0:-}" ]; then
    err "script was executed, not sourced - PATH change is local to this subshell"
    err "to update your shell, run: source $_SETUP_SRC"
  fi
}

_hybrid_setup_main
unset -f _hybrid_setup_main step ok err 2>/dev/null
unset _SETUP_SRC

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build hybrid_qemu out-of-tree under build/qemu/.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SRC="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
BUILD="$ROOT/build/qemu"

if [[ ! -x "$SRC/configure" ]]; then
  echo "missing $SRC/configure - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "$BUILD"

if [[ ! -f "$BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
  (cd "$BUILD" && "$SRC/configure" \
    --target-list=riscv64-softmmu \
    --enable-plugins \
    --disable-werror \
    --disable-libnfs \
    --disable-werror)
fi

make -C "$BUILD" CFLAGS="-Wno-int-conversion -Wno-error=redundant-decls" -j64

# Build vsim inside the env-stage container image.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"

  echo "missing $VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2

fi

cd "$VSIM_DIR"

podman build --target env -t vsim-env:latest .
podman run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work localhost/vsim-env:latest ./build.sh

    if [ -f "$PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile" ]; then

  ok "PATH updated (this shell only)"

You're right — they should move. Honest scope check before I touch the vsim submodule:

**What moves:** `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/include/hybrid/{state_abi.h, state_abi_check.h, insn_match.h, reg_names.h}` → `hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/`

**Updates needed:**
- `qemu_plugin/Makefile`: `HYBRID_INCLUDE` becomes `../include` (cleaner — no longer reaching into vsim)
- `hybrid_vsim/CMakeLists.txt:455`: 1 path
- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`: 8 paths (lines 12, 41, 50, 56, 62, 71, 77, 86)
  - All currently `${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include` → would become `${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include` (vsim is at `hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/`, so `..` is `hybrid_sim/`)

**Tradeoff:** vsim stops being standalone-buildable from a fresh clone of just `hybrid_vsim` — it now requires a sibling `../include/` dir (i.e. requires the hybrid_sim workspace). That matches the new architecture (hybrid_sim owns the contract) but is a real behavior change for anyone doing isolated vsim work.

Two options:
1. **Hard move** (recommended, matches the architecture): paths become `../include`, vsim is workspace-only.
2. **Cache var fallback**: introduce `HYBRID_INCLUDE_DIR` cache var defaulting to `../include`, override-able to old in-tree path. Preserves standalone vsim build.

Which do you want?

# Out-of-tree build for the QEMU TCG plugin libhybrid_handoff.so. Standalone
# from the vsim CMake build because the plugin links against host QEMU's
# headers + glib, neither of which exist inside the vsim build container.
#
# Usage:
#   make QEMU_ROOT=/local/nick/qemu_v5
#
# Output: libhybrid_handoff.so in this directory.

QEMU_ROOT ?= /local/nick/qemu_v5
CC        ?= gcc

GLIB_CFLAGS := $(shell pkg-config --cflags glib-2.0)
GLIB_LIBS   := $(shell pkg-config --libs   glib-2.0)

CFLAGS  := -O2 -fPIC -Wall -Wextra -std=c11
HYBRID_INCLUDE ?= ../hybrid_vsim/hybrid/include
INCLUDES := -I$(HYBRID_INCLUDE) -I$(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu $(GLIB_CFLAGS)
LDFLAGS := -shared
LIBS    := $(GLIB_LIBS)

.PHONY: all clean

all: libhybrid_handoff.so

libhybrid_handoff.so: hybrid_handoff.c \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/state_abi.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/state_abi_check.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/insn_match.h
	@if [ ! -f $(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h ]; then \
	    echo "error: qemu-plugin.h not under $(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu/"; \
	    echo "       set QEMU_ROOT=<path> on the make command line"; \
	    exit 1; \
	fi
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCLUDES) $(LDFLAGS) -o $@ $< $(LIBS)

clean:
	rm -f libhybrid_handoff.so
